title: Daily Briefing — May 05
headline: Tech leads, defensives lag, but bond cracks warn: flows say patience over chasing.
body: Right, so big picture: despite a sea of red and -0.4% on SPY ($718.01), equities are still above their 20 MA (SPY: 701.45). Tech remains the strongest horse in the race — NVDA ($198.48), MSFT ($413.62), and GOOGL ($383.25) all hugging or riding above daily MAs, while Energy and Real Estate show some resilience. VIX is drifting lower (17.69), volatility is fading — but don't let that lull you.

Sector rotation is clear: XLK (Tech, $162.05) is leading with RS 110.3 and momentum 102.2, but watch weakening XLE (Energy), XLU (Utilities), and XLRE (Real Estate). Staples (XLP: $83.54), Industrials (XLI: $170.98), and Financials (XLF: $51.58) are lagging with HIGH risk tagged — the canary isn't singing, it's coughing. Defensive sectors are just not seeing real bid here.

Contrarian warning: Fear & Greed back in 'Greed' at 62.2 — not extreme, but notable considering we were at 23.7 only a month ago. Bonds are breaking down hard: TLT ($84.96) triggered below all MAs, HYG ($79.80) and LQD ($108.25) rolling over. R001 triggered: if bonds freak out, everything breaks. Don't chase. Abundance mindset, wait for mean reversion or flows to reset.
callout_body:
SPY: Support at 701.45 (20 MA), resistance at 718.01
NVDA: Mean reversion zone 198.27 (20 MA)
XLK: Watch 152.92 (20 MA) for momentum shifts
TLT/HYG/LQD: Reduce equity exposure until bonds base
XLF: High risk, lagging — financials are canary in coal mine
Fear & Greed: 75+ = reduce, 25- = opportunity (we're in a 'not-chasing' zone)
action_items: Move any trailing stops up on tech (XLK, NVDA, MSFT); do NOT add fresh longs here — wait for a proper pullback or mean reversion to 20 MA.
Reduce exposure to cyclicals and lagging financials (XLF, HYG/LQD flag), and monitor bond ETF action — any further breakdown means defensive stance.
closing_quote: If bonds freak out, everything breaks. Simple as that. Patience, react, don't predict.
